sections.paragraph:
pl. מְקֹומֹות m. (but fem. however, Job 20:9 Gen 18:24); from the root קוּם No. 2, to stand.

(1) a place (prop. a station, from standing, existing) [“Arab. مَقَامُ Eth. ምቅዋም፡ id. Phœn. מקם a place, a town”], Gen 1:9 24:23 , 25 Gen 24:25 28:11 , 17 Gen 28:17and frequently. Followed by a genit. the place of any one is his abode, habitation, Gen 29:26 30:25 Num 24:11 Jud. 11:19 2Sa 15:19, etc. Poet. Job 16:18, אַל־יְהִי מָקֹום לְזַעֲקָתִי “let there be no place (or abiding) to my outcry;” let it never delay, but let my cry come without tarrying to God. Followed by relat. אֲשֶׁר it is often put in const. st. מְקֹום אֲשֶׁר (the place which), Lev 4:33 14:13 Jer 22:12, (on the other hand מָקֹום אֲשֶׁר Jos 1:3 Jer 13:7 1Sa 20:19); also before זֶה relative, Psa 104:8 and with the relative omitted, Job 18:21, מְקֹום (אֲשֶׁר) לֹא יָדַע אֵל “the habitation (of a man who) knows not God.”-Sometimes מְקֹום אֲשֶׁר in which place, put periphrastically for where (elsewhere בַּאֲשֶׁר, אֲשֶׁר), Est 4:3 8:17 Ecc 11:3 Eze 6:13 like the Syr. ܐܰܬܰܪ ܕ. Adv. for in the place, Isa 33:21, and perhaps Hos 2:1 (compare Arab. مكان, Syr. ܕܘܒܬܐ a place, and adv. loco).

(2) a town, a village (Germ. Ortfchaft). מְקֹום שְׁכֶם the town of Shechem, Gen 12:6 18:24 .
